data.title: US Foods awarded $130.4M Defense Logistics Agency contract
data.description:
• US Foods (NYSE:USFD) has been awarded a maximum $130.4 million contract for full-line food and beverage items.
  • This is a 266-day bridge contract, with the ordering period end date being Aug. 30, 2025.
  • The contracting activity is the Defense Logistics Agency Troop Support, Philadelphia, Pennsylvania.

data.title: Acer’s November revenue up 20.9% M/M to NT$22.75B as Chromebook sales surges 54%
data.description:
• Acer (OTC:ACEYY) has reported consolidated revenues of NT$22.75B for November, up 20.9% from NT$18.82B in October 2024.

  • Year-to-November revenues totaled NT$240.16B, up 10.4% Y/Y.

  • Revenues from the personal computers and display business grew 11.0% Y/Y in November and 9.4% Y/Y year-to-November.
  • Chromebooks were up 54.1% Y/Y in November, signaling strong demand.
  • Businesses other than computers and displays contributed 26.8% of the group's total revenues in November and 28.3% year-to-November.
  • Bifurcating Acer's public subsidiaries' revenue: Altos Computing Inc. up 3.3% Y/Y in November and 64.4% Y/Y year-to-November; Acer ITS Inc. revenues 59.1% Y/Y in November and 35.3% Y/Y year-to-November.

  • Two subsidiaries, Acerpure and Protrade Applied Materials, began trading on Taipei Exchange Emerging Market, while Winking Studios listed on LSE AIM and SGX Catalyst.

data.title: European markets edged higher as investors weigh geopolitical risks
data.description:
London (UKX) +0.4%

Germany (DAX:IND) +0.1%

France (CAC:IND) +0.7%

The pan-European Stoxx 600 (STOXX) rose +0.3% on Monday, as investors weighed geopolitical turmoil in the Middle East after the ousting of Syrian President Bashar al-Assad over the weekend. Focus also remains on the European Central Bank, which is expected to cut interest rates again this week.

U.S. stock futures were little changed Sunday night after the S&P 500 and Nasdaq Composite posted their third straight winning week.

Coming up in the session: A lighter day on charts.

In the bond market, the U.S. 10-year Treasury yield was up 1 basis points to 4.16%.

Germany's 10-year yield was down less than 1 basis point at 2.11%.

UK's 10-year yield was down less than 1 basis point to 4.28%.

Currencies: (EUR:USD) (GBP:USD) (CHF:USD)

ETFs: (EWG), (GF), (EWI), (EWQ), (FGM), (DAX), (FLGR), (FXB), (EWU), (FKU), (EWUS), (FLGB), (GREK)

data.title: Earnings Snapshot: Hello Group tops Q3 estimates; initiates Q4 revenue outlook
Google Payment placed under CFPB supervision, company hits back with lawsuit
data.description:
The Consumer Financial Protection Bureau has established supervisory authority over Alphabet's (NASDAQ:GOOG) (NASDAQ:GOOGL) Google Payment, citing "reasonable cause" to determine that its conduct poses risks to consumers. The company immediately sued the CFPB, claiming regulatory overreach.

The watchdog pointed to Google's practices in investigating allegedly erroneous transactions, and its conduct in connection with the prevention of fraudulent and unauthorized transactions.

The CFPB clarified that its order "does not constitute a finding that the entity has engaged in wrongdoing."

In response, Google sued the CFPB, claiming the order would subject it to "burdensome supervision" and is "in excess of statutory authority."

The company previously argued that the designation was only warranted if there is a "meaningful possibility of substantial harm to consumers." It also claimed that the CFPB relied on "33 cherry-picked complaints" involving peer-to-peer payments that it discontinued earlier this year.

The CFPB said it relied on 267 consumer complaints, and the regulatory framework for the designation does not specify the degree of risk needed to justify supervision.

"Further, Google's discontinuation of the Google Pay App and the P2P payment platform is not a basis to refrain from designating Google for supervision," it added.

data.title: Hello Group Non-GAAP EPADS of $0.38 beats by $0.12, revenue of $381.1M beats by $15.28M
data.description:
• Hello Group press release (NASDAQ:MOMO): Q3 Non-GAAP EPADS of $0.38 beats by $0.12.
  • Revenue of $381.1M (-12.1% Y/Y) beats by $15.28M.
  • Monthly Active Users on Tantan app were 12.0 million in September 2024, compared to 15.7 million in September 2023.
  • For the Momo app total paying users was 6.9 million for the third quarter of 2024, compared to 7.8 million for the same period last year.
  • Tantan had 0.9 million paying users for the third quarter of 2024 compared to 1.4 million from the year ago period.

  • Business Outlook: For the fourth quarter of 2024, the company expects total net revenues to be between RMB2.56 billion to RMB2.66 billion, representing a decrease of 14.7% to 11.4% year over year.

data.title: Desert Control, Woodland Hills Country Club teams up to deploy water conservation technology
data.description:
• Desert Control (OTC:DRTFF) has signed an agreement through its U.S. subsidiary, Desert Control Americas Inc., with Woodland Hills Country Club for the deployment of Desert Control's proprietary water conservation solution across 43 acres of WHCC's golf course located in Woodland Hills, California.
  • The agreement focuses on an outcome-based business model, generating monthly recurring revenue for Desert Control based on water savings.
  • The contract value could exceed NOK 8.5 million, with a minimum 25% water savings over five years, providing significant economic benefits for Woodland Hills Country Club.
  • The pact includes implementing Desert Control's water conservation technology across all fairways, tees, and roughs of the golf course.
  • The rollout is scheduled to coincide with the club's regular turf aeration and maintenance activities, targeted for April 2025.

data.title: HIVE reports robust operational growth in Nov with 103 BTC mined; HODL grew 67% to 2,713 Bitcoins
data.description:
• HIVE (NASDAQ:HIVE) reports robust operational growth with 103 BTC (BTC-USD) mined and total holdings of 2,713 BTC (BTC-USD), marking a significant 67% Y/Y increase.
  • The company's Bitcoin holdings are valued at $261 million as of November 30, 2024.
  • The company maintained an average mining capacity of 5.3 EH/s with fleet efficiency at 22.3 J/TH, reaching a peak hashrate of 5.7 EH/s.
  • The company achieved an average daily production rate of 3.42 BTC, equivalent to 19.3 Bitcoin per Exahash.
  • The strategic fleet expansion to 15 EH/s by summer 2025 through acquisitions of Avalon and Bitmain miners positions HIVE for substantial revenue growth, with projected annual revenue of $300 million and mining margins exceeding $200 million.

data.title: JinkoSolar files patent infringement lawsuit against VSUN and affiliates
data.description:
• JinkoSolar (NYSE:JKS) has filed a patent infringement action in the U.S. District Court for the Northern District of California against VSUN Solar USA, Toyo Co., and their related entities in the U.S. and abroad for infringement of its patents, the company said on Saturday.
  • "JinkoSolar has earned its leadership position in the global solar module market through significant R&D investments and will vigorously defend its intellectual property against those who sell infringing products," said Louise Lu, a patent litigation lawyer with the Perkins Coie law firm.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Apple to open first flagship retail store in Saudi Arabia in 2026
data.description:
• Apple (NASDAQ:AAPL) Sunday unveiled plans to expand in the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia, starting with the launch of the Apple Store online in summer 2025, and will open its first flagship retail store in 2026.
  • “We’re excited to be expanding here in Saudi Arabia with the launch of the Apple Store online next year, and the first of several flagship Apple Store locations starting in 2026, including an iconic store at the stunning site of Diriyah coming later,” said Tim Cook, Apple’s (AAPL) CEO.
  • Apple’s retail expansion builds on its existing investments and activities in the country. This includes the region’s first Apple Developer Academy, which opened in Riyadh in 2021 in partnership with the Government of the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia, Tuwaiq Academy, and Princess Nourah bint, the
    iPhone maker
     said.
  • Source: Press Release
